channel.title: Mark Hendrickson
channel.description: Essays on user-owned agent memory, personal infrastructure, and building systems that restore sovereignty in an age of AI, crypto, and complexity.

item.title: The diary and the dance
item.description: Creation, whether a startup or a blog post, is a continuous habit of revealing your inner world and tuning into what comes back. The MVP is a continuum. So is everything else.

item.title: AI skepticism is really about faith in humans
item.description: The people who embrace AI encounter its problems more directly than anyone. But they also have the most faith that humans can work through them. The real skepticism isn't about the machines.

item.title: What my agentic stack actually does
item.description: I built an agentic stack to dogfood Neotoma and accelerate my work. A private monorepo with 12+ MCP servers where I cowork with AI agents daily. Neotoma provides the structured memory underneath, letting agents build on prior work across every session.

item.title: When the chain becomes the product
item.description: I joined Blockstack in 2018 for the off-chain developer tools. Over seven years I watched token economics reshape the product direction, push the perceived moment of value perpetually into the future, and replace empirical product development with narrative-driven roadmaps.

item.title: Google's memory agent thinks for itself, trading determinism for insight
item.description: A new open-source memory agent from Google ditches vector databases for LLM consolidation, trading determinism for active pattern discovery. It makes opposite architectural choices from a truth layer on every axis. The strongest architecture may combine both: consolidation above, deterministic state you can audit and revert below.

item.title: Your AI remembers your vibe but not your work
item.description: Claude and ChatGPT now offer memory for free, but what they store are profile snippets, not the details of what you actually worked on. My testing showed stale exports, one-shot access, and platform-gated portability. That is why I am building a truth layer underneath.

item.title: Neotoma is now available as developer release
item.description: Structured agent memory you can inspect, replay, and trust. Open-source, local-first, available via npm for ChatGPT, Claude, and Cursor.

item.title: Agent memory has a truth problem
item.description: Retrieval fits coding and is cheap; it breaks on completeness, consistency, and provenance. Why convergence to structured state is hard, and how Neotoma is designed for it.

item.title: Agent command centers need one source of truth
item.description: Command centers for agents need a single, durable state layer for tasks and visibility. The UI is the dashboard; the layer it reads and writes is the substrate.

item.title: Six agentic trends I'm betting on (and how I might be wrong)
item.description: The structural pressures that underpin my work, and what would invalidate them as the AI industry evolves.

item.title: We're all centaurs now
item.description: The humanness isn't in typing every word. It's in deciding what's worth saying.

item.title: A Bitcoin wallet MCP server for L1 and L2
item.description: The future of crypto wallets is agentic. Wallets that expose execution surfaces let agents monitor, reason, and execute within policy while you keep sovereignty and approve only what exceeds your limits.

item.title: Why agent memory needs more than RAG
item.description: Retrieval for agent memory should be driven by structure, not similarity. Learned hierarchies beat RAG but are brittle. Schema-first design gives the same advantage without putting the LLM in the critical path.

item.title: Building structural barriers that incumbents can't copy
item.description: You don't protect it through secrecy or patents. You build something they structurally can't pursue.

item.title: Agentic retrieval infers. It doesn't guarantee.
item.description: Your AI finds things. It also misses things, overwrites them, and often can't say where an answer came from. Why that happens and what would fix it.

item.title: Barcelona guest floor
item.description: Your own floor in the heart of Barcelona. Quiet street in Gràcia, kitchen, king bed, rooftop terrace. Welcoming to all.

item.title: OpenClaw is powerful at doing but could use some help remembering.
item.description: OpenClaw runs on your machine, clears your inbox, and builds skills. The gap is memory you can verify, query, and fix. A truth layer under it gives you provenance, rollback, and the same result every time you ask.

item.title: Building a truth layer for persistent agent memory
item.description: Agent memory is forgetful. Why I'm building inspectable, structured memory for trustworthy agentic systems.

item.title: Mark Hendrickson
item.description: Building sovereign memory infrastructure for AI agents.

item.title: The Flip Side
item.description: I've been reading The Flip Side since October and find it's an incredibly effective and efficient way to encounter and digest thoughtful opinions...

item.title: Yc W19 Web Developer Contract
item.description: Web developers: The founder of a YC W19 company I know is looking to contract immediately for a project involving React, Node, and MongoDB with...

item.title: The Facebook police state
item.description: Should Facebook really be responsible for policing the misuse of user datahttps://newsroom.fb.com/news/2018/03/forensic-audits-cambridge-analytica/...

item.title: Finding flow beyond distraction
item.description: One my highest priorities over the past several years has been to establish a more frequent state of flowhttps://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flowpsychology...

item.title: Alps Snowboarding Recommendations
item.description: Any recommendations on the best snowboarding destinations in the Alps with easy access from Barcelona and London? 🏂

item.title: The Outline
item.description: This is the most impressive media website I've seen in a while, from a creative design and construction quality point of view:...

item.title: Tutor Catala
item.description: Algú coneix a un tutor de català bo? Estic buscant un per a fer classes d'una hora i mitja a La Dreta per els matis de dimarts i dijous abans de la...

item.title: Syncing Foursquare / Swarm checkins to my website
item.description: Neotoma now backs up all of my Foursquare / Swarm check-ins to my Dropbox account whereupon they're republished instantly to my website on a new check-ins page.

item.title: Dropbox API v2 fix for Neotoma sync server
item.description: As part of my working sprint at IndieWebCamp Berlin today, I managed to fix a show-stopping bug that's been in production for sync-server on Neotoma and run a backup job for my latest Foursquare / Swarm check-ins.

item.title: Publishing to my website instantly with Dropbox
item.description: My website's content is now populated automatically via Dropbox using Neotoma personal server and publishing software, reducing the friction to publishing, keeping data in sync, and paving the way for content aggregation.

item.title: New Node.js repos for environment management, deployment and proxying
item.description: While working more or less full-time on Neotoma since last summer, I've created a few repositories that are hopefully useful as modules to other Node.js apps in general.

item.title: Touch Bar Autosuggest
item.description: "Hmmm what I've been doing is that I wanted to be a good friend to my life and my life is so far away" - My Touch Bar autosuggest

item.title: Applying for the Spanish non-lucrative residence visa
item.description: Just over a year ago, I moved to Barcelona in Spain to live abroad for the first time. I booked a flight from San Francisco with little else in terms of preparation, an intentionally rash decision to experience what it would be like to land in a new country and improvise the establishment of a life there.

item.title: Bringing it all back home
item.description: > "Now, I been in jail when all my mail showed, that a man can't give his address out to bad company" - Bob Dylan, Absolutely Sweet Marie Last summer...

item.title: 11 Reasons Why I Won't Click On Your Link Bait
item.description: 1. It blatantly panders to my predispositions. 2. I presume it was written by an underpaid intern. 3. I already saw the content on Reddit. 4. I'm...

item.title: Kanban
item.description: One of the most important aspects of product design and management is prioritization. There are only so many resources at a company's disposal at any...

item.title: Success projection for startups
item.description: Startups often get so consumed by their day-to-day challenges that they do a poor job actually projecting their longer-term success or lack thereof....

item.title: Three types of design
item.description: People tend to use the word "design" very loosely when talking about product, but it's actually quite important to distinguish between the types of...

item.title: The Post-MVP Fork in the Road
item.description: A lot of startups rightly aim to release a minimum viable product MVP as their first initiative. The goal of an MVP helps a team rally around a...

item.title: Does your product provide instant gratification?
item.description: Over the past year, and mostly as a result of my Plancast post-mortem, I've spoken with dozens of entrepreneurs who are working on events-related...

item.title: A Post-Mortem for Plancast
item.description: Nearly three years ago, I left my position at TechCrunchhttp://techcrunch.com/2009/03/10/hendrickson-were-gonna-miss-you/ to start my own Internet...

item.title: Share frequency
item.description: When designing a social network that depends on users to contribute content from which they'll collectively derive value, one must consider certain...

item.title: Homesteading on the indie web
item.description: I had the pleasure of attending IndieWebCamphttp://indiewebcamp.com/ in Portland last month, a BarCamp-style conference where techies get together to...

item.title: Content
item.description: People are particular about the forms of communication they employ when expressing themselves, through social networks or any other media, because...

item.title: Relationship
item.description: When people connect with others on a given social network, they are conscientious about whom they will connect with, because an exchange of...

item.title: Three pillars of social networking
item.description: Social networking is a precondition for new modes of information exchange, not an end in and of itself. By definition, a social network is simply a...

item.title: How to pitch a tech blogger
item.description: I've been asked privately quite a few times over the last couple of years how one should pitch their startup to a tech blog like...

item.title: Some late-night ideas for Blippy
item.description: I feel a certain kinship towards the founders of Blippyhttp://blippy.com/. Not because I know them well I've met Philip Kaplan aka Pud only once but...

item.title: Plancast in public beta
item.description: I'm happy to announce that the site I've been working on for the past half year or so - Plancasthttp://plancast.com/ - is now available in public...

item.title: Facebook's social graph
item.description: The social graph on Facebook has been the company's biggest asset, but over time it has become perhaps its biggest liability as well. When users want...

item.title: Worldly Developments
item.description: I'm happy to announce some exciting news regarding my startup. I've officially incorporated as Worldly Developmentshttp://worldlydevelopments.com/...

item.title: The web needs its own App Store
item.description: I've been thinking a bit about the Apple App Store recently, perhaps because I'm starting to think more about developing a mobile application that...

item.title: Narrowing Scope
item.description: Since my last post, I've narrowed the scope of my project down a great deal. The main reason was to make it more accessible to first-time users who...

item.title: The unfolding legacy of Twitter for software design
item.description: The immense amount of ink spilled for Twitter these days signals two main things. First, that web innovation in general is going through a...

item.title: My first week as an entrepreneur
item.description: Today marks the completion of my first week working on my first startup/getting-the-lay-of-the-land. So, I thought it would be a good time to share...

item.title: Getting the lay of the land
item.description: As Erick Schonfeld was kind enough to announce in a post on TechCrunch last week, I've left my position at TechCrunch to build and run my own startup.
